title: Wire isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget into the write-guard hook (D1b)
action: Per D1(b), call guards.isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget(root, absoluteTargetPath) inside hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs's main() dispatch, at the same point resolveCompanionMountedRelPath/describeCrossWorktreeTarget already run (around lines 791 and 804 for Bash and Edit/Write targets respectively) -- for each resolved rel target, if isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget returns true, deny the write with a typed, actionable message BEFORE calling guards.checkWrite (this is a new hard-fail-closed check per D3, not a checkWrite branch, since it needs the raw absolute target path guards.checkWrite doesn't receive). The denial message MUST redirect to `bee worktree new --with-companion` as a FRESH worktree (per D4 -- do not word it as upgrading the CURRENT worktree in place; it cannot be, per plan.md's Rollback Plan note). Per D5, this check must run unconditionally regardless of gate_bypass -- it lives in the hook, never consult .bee/config.json gate_bypass at all (existing hook-level guards in this file never do). This cell is `behavior_change: true` -- a real write that used to succeed can now be refused. Prove it red-first: write a test where a plain nested checkout write while concurrent currently SUCCEEDS (today, pre-wiring), confirm that red/passing-when-it-shouldn't state, THEN wire the check and confirm the same write now correctly denies, while a solo-session write to the same target and a verified-companion-mount write both remain allowed exactly as wcg-1's helper already proves. Do not modify guards.mjs itself in this cell -- isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget already exists and is proven; call it, do not change it.

truths: A write into a plain nested checkout inside a shared checkout, or into a verified companion mount by an UNVERIFIED path, is denied when isConcurrentMode() is true
The exact same writes remain ALLOWED when no other session is concurrently live (D6 backward-compat, matches wcg-1's own no-op proof)
The denial message names `bee worktree new --with-companion` as the fix, worded as opening a fresh worktree, never as upgrading the current one in place

artifacts.substantive: new denial branch calling guards.isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget before checkWrite, with a typed, actionable refusal message
red-before/green-after regression rows proving the new denial fires only when concurrent, and only for a genuinely shared/nested target

prohibitions: Do not modify guards.mjs's isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget in this cell
Do not gate this check on gate_bypass -- it is hook-level per D5, never bypassed
Do not word the refusal as an in-place upgrade of the current worktree -- always a fresh `bee worktree new --with-companion`

regen_obligation_ack: release_manifest.mjs --check stays dropped from verify for the same pre-existing, already-filed, unrelated file-mode-drift reason recorded on wcg-1 (2026-07-23 friction: mode differ on ~500 files, main + every worktree). The action still runs release_manifest.mjs --write for real content-hash regen of the touched files.

trace.outcome: Wired guards.isSharedNestedCheckoutTarget into bee-write-guard.mjs's Edit/Write and Bash dispatch (both hook copies) as a hard fail-closed pre-checkWrite refusal (D1b/D3/D5): a write into a genuinely shared nested checkout another live session can also reach, not covered by a verified companion marker, is denied with a typed refusal directing to a FRESH bee worktree new --with-companion (D4). Verified companion mounts and solo sessions stay allowed (D6). Never consults gate_bypass.
trace.friction: release_manifest.mjs --write rewrites ~510 mode-drift lines plus 2 unrelated pre-existing .bee-render.json content-hash entries; per the cell regen_obligation_ack the wholesale write was reverted and only the 2 touched-file sha256 entries were surgically applied. Same pre-existing mode-drift friction already filed on wcg-1 (2026-07-23).
trace.verify_output: node --test: tests 1 / pass 1 / fail 0 (ALL PASS, 82 rows incl. new wcg-2 rows 78-82); ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger. exit 0.
